title: Lisa Smart MP - Hazel Grove Liberal Democrats
headings: h2: Building a brighter future for our area
h2: An MP with track record
h2: With the experience to get things done
h2: Lisa cares for our communities in Hazel Grove constituency.
text_excerpt: Lisa Smart MP - Hazel Grove Liberal Democrats Skip to main content Hazel Grove Liberal Democrats Vote Lib Dem Get involved Local news Our campaigns Campaign wins Lisa Smart MP Our local teams Our party Contact Donate Join Donate Join Get involved Get emails Display a poster Andrew Stunell Fund Our campaigns Protect Our Community Park at Tangshutt Fields An additional hospital for Stockport NHS dentists Fair adoption pay Save our green belt Community-led housing Stop sewage Marple's World Heritage Speed Camera Campaign Illegal off-road biking Save Hazel Grove's NatWest Branch Save post office services in High Lane Save Stockport Post Office Campaign wins Specialist status for Stepping Hill Save our ticket offices Offerton fire crews saved New leisure hub for Marple Preserve the Green Belt Save Central Library Preserving Chadkirk Chapel Saving the 375 bus Rail services on the Rose Hill line Our local teams Bredbury & Woodley Bredbury Green & Romiley Hazel Grove Manor Marple North Marple South & High Lane Norbury & Woodsmoor Offerton Our party What we stand for Get updates Lisa Smart MP Building a brighter future for our area A strong local champion Born and brought up in the North West, Lisa lives in Romiley with her partner Ed, who grew up in High Lane, and their rescue dog Bonnie. Lisa cares deeply about Hazel Grove Constituency and its people, working as an active volunteer locally. Her voluntary roles include serving as a trustee for the New Horizons Canal Boat for disabled people and their carers in Marple, working as a volunteer for the Woodley & Romiley Towpath Team, and being a trustee of the Barrack Hill Education Trust. An MP with track record Lisa has been a very active and successful campaigner, and is now taking that into her work as an MP. Campaigns she has been at the heart of include securing Specialist Status for Stepping Hill Hospital, saving the Rose Hill to Manchester rail service, keeping the 380/381 bus running, and improving road safety across the borough with a campaign to get 20mph speed limits for residential roads and another seeking changes in the current rules for fixed speed cameras. Previously serving as an elected Councillor for Bredbury Green & Romiley, Lisa trained and mentored other councillors from around the country. Other areas Lisa is campaigning hard on are the climate emergency and the need for us to protect green space, tackle air pollution and protect wildlife. With the experience to get things done The former Chief Executive of an international education development charity, Lisa has been a school governor for more than 16 years. She is committed to making sure young people get the best start in life and their voices are heard. The first of her family to go to university, Lisa graduated from Durham University with a degree in Mathematics followed by an MBA from the London Business School. Lisa is known locally and nationally for being gutsy and getting things done, whether it is through her voluntary roles, by working as a Director of the Joseph Rowntree Reform Trust or though convincing Council bosses to give Asset of Community Value status to protect local green space. Never afraid to stand up for what is right and fair, Lisa also knows how to demand better use of government and local council money, with 12 years of experience looking after pension funds and charity investments for UK, US and Canadian clients. Lisa cares for our communities in Hazel Grove constituency. Support Lisa now! The Liberal Democrats will use your contact details to send you information on the topics you have requested.
